title: Error HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
description:
### Company or project name
### Describe what's wrong
In version 25.8.7.3, as well as after updating to version 25.8.8.26 and after updating to version 25.9.2.1
I also checked on the installation from scratch of version 25.9.2.1.
In the logs, spam errors like:
2025.10.01 12:59:43.295837 [ 10683 ] {06b6e074-ea6a-407c-b6c0-19fd6520ec11} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
2025.10.01 12:59:45.202751 [ 10683 ] {233d8d16-fca3-4638-a092-3d44c3d5d97c} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
2025.10.01 12:59:46.735494 [ 10683 ] {b20489da-fb1d-4a06-a1a2-b038fb4f0a9b} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Version 25.9.2.1
dbeaver was used as the client
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tabletest ON CLUSTER 'default'
(
id UInt32,
timestamp DateTime,
user_id UInt32,
message String,
large_data String
)
ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/{shard}/{table}', '{replica}')
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(timestamp)
ORDER BY (user_id, timestamp);
INSERT INTO tabletest
SELECT
number AS id,
now() + number * 10 AS timestamp,
rand() % 1000 AS user_id,
concat('Message ', toString(number)) AS message,
randomString(200) AS large_data
FROM numbers(1_000_000);
select count(*) from tabletest;
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
2025.10.01 12:59:43.295837 [ 10683 ] {06b6e074-ea6a-407c-b6c0-19fd6520ec11} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
2025.10.01 12:59:45.202751 [ 10683 ] {233d8d16-fca3-4638-a092-3d44c3d5d97c} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
2025.10.01 12:59:46.735494 [ 10683 ] {b20489da-fb1d-4a06-a1a2-b038fb4f0a9b} <Error> HTTPServerRequest: Request stream is shared by multiple threads. HTTP keep alive is not possible. Use count 2
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_activity (activity_id UInt64, user_id UInt32, activity_time DateTime, activity_type VARCHAR(20), details VARCHAR(255)) PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR_MONTH FROM activity_time) ORDER BY (user_id, activity_time);

INSERT INTO user_activity SELECT number AS activity_id, MOD(rand(), 1000) AS user_id, NOW() + number * INTERVAL '5' SECOND AS activity_time, (CASE WHEN MOD(number, 3) = 0 THEN 'login' WHEN MOD(number, 3) = 1 THEN 'logout' ELSE 'purchase' END) AS activity_type, CONCAT('Activity details for ', CAST(number AS CHAR)) AS details FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS number FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 100);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS product_sales (sale_id UInt32, product_id UInt32, sale_time DateTime, quantity UInt16, price FLOAT) PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR_MONTH FROM sale_time) ORDER BY (product_id, sale_time);

INSERT INTO product_sales SELECT number AS sale_id, MOD(rand(), 500) AS product_id, NOW() + number * INTERVAL '2' SECOND AS sale_time, (MOD(rand(), 10) + 1) AS quantity, (MOD(rand(), 100) + 1) * 1.5 AS price FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS number FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 50);

SELECT ua.user_id, COUNT(*) AS activity_count, SUM(ps.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM user_activity ua LEFT JOIN product_sales ps ON ua.user_id = ps.product_id WHERE ua.activity_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '1' DAY GROUP BY ua.user_id HAVING total_quantity > 5 ORDER BY activity_count DESC;
key_bug_features: Request stream is shared by multiple threads
